messages.content: Can you break down "化妆演员" into morphemes?

Sure! "化妆演员" can be broken down into the following morphemes:

化 (huà) - to transform or change
妆 (zhuāng) - makeup or cosmetics
演员 (yǎnyuán) - actor or actress

So, "化妆演员" can be translated as "makeup actor" or "makeup artist."
"着ぐるみ"

"着ぐるみ" is a Japanese word. It can be broken down into the following morphemes:

着 (ki/chi) - to wear or put on
ぐるみ (gurumi) - a type of costume or suit, typically referring to a full body animal or character costume

So, "着ぐるみ" can be translated as "wearing costume" or "animal costume."
"布偶裝"

"布偶裝" is a Chinese term. It can be broken down into the following morphemes:

布 (bù) - cloth or fabric
偶 (ǒu) - puppet or doll
裝 (zhuāng) - costume or attire

So, "布偶裝" can be translated as "puppet costume" or "doll outfit."
"탈인형"

"탈인형" is a Korean word. It can be broken down into the following morphemes:

탈 (tal) - mask or disguise
인형 (inhyeong) - doll or puppet

So, "탈인형" can be translated as "mask doll" or "mask puppet."
"洗澡"

"洗澡" is a Chinese term. It can be broken down into the following morphemes:

洗 (xǐ) - to wash or cleanse
澡 (zǎo) - bath or shower

So, "洗澡" can be translated as "to wash or take a bath."
臺灣體育吉祥物

"臺灣體育吉祥物" is a Chinese term referring to the "Taiwanese sports mascot." It can be broken down into the following morphemes:

臺灣 (Táiwān) - Taiwan
體育 (tǐyù) - sports or physical education
吉祥物 (jíxiángwù) - mascot or symbol of good fortune

So, "臺灣體育吉祥物" can be translated as "Taiwanese sports mascot."
扮演

"扮演" is a Chinese term. It can be broken down into the following morphemes:

扮 (bàn) - to dress up or portray
演 (yǎn) - to perform or act

So, "扮演" can be translated as "to play a role" or "to portray."
"台灣吉祥物的｢奇妙樣貌巾 醜到國際引熱議 網友驚呼快嚇死!"

The phrase you provided seems to be a news headline or sentence about Taiwan's mascot having a unique or interesting appearance that has sparked international discussion and shocked online users. The translation of the phrase is as follows:

"The fascinating appearance of Taiwan's mascot scarf: Shockingly ugly, igniting international discussions, and online users exclaiming they are almost scared to death!"

Please note that the translation may not capture the full context or nuances of the original text.
"臺灣吉祥物的媒體集"

"臺灣吉祥物的媒體集" can be translated as "Taiwan's collection of media about mascots." 

The phrase refers to a gathering or compilation of various media content, such as articles, videos, or publications, related to Taiwanese mascots.
"家扶兒寶娃娃"

"家扶兒寶娃娃" is a Chinese term. It can be broken down into the following morphemes:

家 (jiā) - home or family
扶 (fú) - to support or assist
兒 (ér) - child
寶 (bǎo) - treasure or precious
娃娃 (wáwá) - doll or baby

So, "家扶兒寶娃娃" can be translated as "family support for precious children" or "dolls/babies that support children in the family."
"熊讚"

"熊讚" is a Chinese term. It can be broken down into the following morphemes:

熊 (xióng) - bear
讚 (zàn) - p raise or endorsement

So, "熊讚" can be translated as "bear praise" or "bear endorsement." It could refer to a positive evaluation or approval of bears, potentially in the context of a mascot or character.
